summary: Ukrainian air defenses shot down 115 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera and Iltamys models, across the north, south and east. Tactical Russian aviation conducted guided bomb strikes in Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia regions, while Ukrainian ground forces reported advances in Kharkiv and heavy fighting in Dobropillya. Civilian infrastructure suffered multiple drone attacks in Belgorod and Kharkiv oblasts, causing injuries and property damage.
key_points: Ukrainian air defenses downed 115 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera and Iltamys types, across multiple fronts, with seven strike hits recorded at five locations (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bomb strikes on Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia regions (Source: @kpszsu/62425, @kpszsu/62428).

Ukrainian assault units of the 82nd Motor Rifle Regiment broke resistance and expelled the 120th Territorial Defense Brigade from Shesterovka, liberating the settlement (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Heavy fighting continued in the Dobropillya direction as Russian forces advanced toward Shevchenko, Rodynske and Novoaleksandrovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
In Belgorod Oblast, an FPV drone detonated in Novaya Tavolzhanka wounding a civilian; additional drone strikes wounded civilians in Shebekino and near Krasivo (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Multiple UAVs were shot down near Moscow, with up to 10 destroyed in Tula Oblast and six in Kaluga, causing damage to civilian structures (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Ukrainian forces reported small‑arms battles and basement clearing in Zapsellya, Kondratovka, Ivolzhanske and surrounding areas of the Sumy direction (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Civilian casualties reported in Kherson Oblast: two wounded and one killed by Ukrainian strikes, with shelling covering the region's depth (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian air defenses achieved a major success, downing 115 hostile UAVs across the country, while Russian tactical aviation intensified guided bomb strikes in Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia. Ukrainian ground units made notable gains in Kharkiv, pushing out Ukrainian Territorial Defense elements, even as heavy fighting persisted in Dobropillya and surrounding sectors. Civilian areas in Belgorod and Kharkiv oblasts suffered multiple drone attacks, resulting in injuries and property damage.

points: Ukrainian forces of the 82nd Motor Rifle Regiment, 69th MRD and 6th Army expelled the 120th Territorial Defense Brigade from Shesterovka, liberating the settlement and continuing small‑arms engagements in Ohrimovka and Volchansk forest (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).

In the Sumy direction, Ukrainian assault units advanced on twenty sectors up to 450 m, clearing basements in Zapsellya and Kondratovka and engaging in small‑arms battles in Ivolzhanske (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).

Ukrainian units reported ongoing heavy fighting in Dobropillya, pushing Russian forces toward Shevchenko, Rodynske and Novoaleksandrovka, while also contesting Vasylevka and Myrne (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).

Ukrainian air defense units, supported by radar and mobile fire groups, intercepted 115 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera and Iltamys, across northern, southern and eastern sectors (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Ukrainian forces conducted basement clearing operations in Zapsellya and Kondratovka, indicating a focus on eliminating entrenched enemy positions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Ukrainian artillery and electronic warfare units engaged enemy UAVs launched from Bryansk, Kursk, Shatalovo, Oryol, and other directions, maintaining airspace denial (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Ukrainian defensive measures intensified in northern oblasts, with SBU and other units implementing heightened security protocols (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30376).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia, targeting front‑line areas and infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/62425, @kpszsu/62428).
Russian forces in Belgorod Oblast used FPV drones, causing civilian injuries in Novaya Tavolzhanka, Shebekino and near Krasivo (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).

Ukrainian reports indicate Russian UAV attacks near Moscow, with up to 10 Shahed drones downed in Tula and six in Kaluga, causing damage to an educational facility and an apartment building (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Russian units in the Kharkiv direction suffered losses, with the 120th Territorial Defense Brigade forced to retreat from Shesterovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Russian forces continued to target civilian heavy trucks within range in Belgorod, indicating a systematic interdiction campaign (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
In the Kherson region, Russian‑controlled areas experienced Ukrainian strikes that wounded two civilians and killed one, reflecting ongoing contestation (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Russian air defense engaged multiple UAV swarms launched from multiple oblasts, indicating a broad, coordinated aerial offensive (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Ukrainian forces reported small‑arms battles and basement clearing in Zapsellya and Kondratovka (Sumy direction), advancing up to 450 m on twenty sectors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Heavy fighting persisted in Dobropillya, with Russian advances toward Shevchenko, Rodynske and Novoaleksandrovka, and contested villages Vasylevka and Myrne (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Ukrainian assault units in the Kharkiv direction liberated Shesterovka and continued engagements in Ohrimovka and Volchansk forest (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Ukrainian reports indicated ongoing combat in Konstantinovka, with heavy fire on enemy fortifications but no decisive shift (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Ukrainian forces noted increased focus on Prymorske, Stepnogorsk and Malaya Tokmachka in Zaporozhye front, with power outages suggesting successful strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Russian tactical bombings targeted Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia, affecting eastern operational depth (Source: @kpszsu/62425, @kpszsu/62428).
Ukrainian units reported continued small‑arms engagements in Ivolzhanske and surrounding areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Ukrainian air defenses downed numerous hostile UAVs launched from Kursk, Shatalovo, Oryol, Bryansk and other northern locations (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Ukrainian security agencies increased protective measures in northern oblasts, reflecting heightened threat perception (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30376).
UAV attacks near Moscow resulted in 10 drones destroyed in Tula and six in Kaluga, with civilian infrastructure damage (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Russian UAVs were reported over Bryansk, with three civilian casualties in Unecha and a wounded civilian in Belaya Beryozka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Ukrainian forces reported successful interception of UAVs over the north, maintaining air superiority (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Russian air defense engaged multiple UAV swarms, indicating a coordinated northern aerial offensive (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Ukrainian units continued to monitor and respond to UAV activity along the northern front, with mobile fire groups active (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Ukrainian forces reported civilian casualties in Kherson Oblast: two wounded and one killed by Ukrainian strikes, indicating ongoing combat across the region's depth (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Russian FPV drone attacks wounded civilians in Belgorod Oblast villages Novaya Tavolzhanka and Shebekino, and near Krasivo (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Ukrainian air defense intercepted UAVs over southern sectors, contributing to the total of 115 downed drones (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Russian tactical bombings in Zaporizhzhia targeted southern operational areas, affecting logistics and civilian infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/62428).
Ukrainian reports indicated continued small‑arms battles and clearing operations in southern villages of the Zaporozhye front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Ukrainian forces claimed to have struck a Russian command post in occupied Kherson region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Ukrainian defensive measures intensified in southern oblasts following increased drone activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down 115 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera and Iltamys, across north, south and east, with seven strike hits recorded at five locations (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided bomb strikes on Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia, employing precision munitions against front‑line targets (Source: @kpszsu/62425, @kpszsu/62428).
UAV attacks near Moscow resulted in 10 drones downed in Tula and six in Kaluga, causing damage to civilian structures (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV sightings and interceptions over Kharkiv, Belgorod and other border oblasts, indicating a high‑tempo aerial contest (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Russian FPV drone usage in Belgorod caused civilian injuries, showing diversification of aerial attack methods (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Ukrainian air defense integrated radar, electronic warfare and mobile fire groups to counter UAV swarms from multiple directions (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Continued UAV activity over northern oblasts prompted heightened security protocols by SBU and other agencies (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30376).

key_developments: Ukrainian air defenses downed 115 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera and Iltamys, across multiple fronts, with seven strike hits recorded at five locations (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bomb strikes on Donetsk, intensifying the aerial campaign in the eastern theater (Source: @kpszsu/62425).
Ukrainian assault units of the 82nd Motor Rifle Regiment expelled the 120th Territorial Defense Brigade from Shesterovka, liberating the settlement (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Heavy fighting continued in Dobropillya as Russian forces advanced toward Shevchenko, Rodynske and Novoaleksandrovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
FPV drone attacks in Belgorod Oblast wounded civilians in Novaya Tavolzhanka and Shebekino, highlighting cross‑border drone threats (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
UAV attacks near Moscow resulted in 10 drones destroyed in Tula and six in Kaluga, causing damage to an educational facility and an apartment building (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
Civilian casualties in Kherson Oblast: two wounded and one killed by Ukrainian strikes, indicating deep‑penetration combat (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30353).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are escalating aerial pressure through guided bombings and increased FPV drone usage, targeting both military and civilian sites in Donetsk, Zaporizhzhia and border oblasts. Ukrainian air defenses have demonstrated resilience, intercepting a large volume of hostile UAVs, but the persistent UAV threat from northern launch points remains a critical vulnerability. Ground combat remains intense in the eastern and southern sectors, with Ukrainian advances offset by Russian counter‑offensives and heavy fighting around key logistics nodes.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is bolstering air defense integration, deploying radar, electronic warfare and mobile fire groups to counter UAV swarms, while also reinforcing ground operations in Kharkiv and Dobropillya. Russian command appears to be leveraging tactical aviation and drone strikes to pressure front‑line positions and civilian infrastructure, especially in border regions like Belgorod. Internationally, the United States approved a $108 million Hawk air‑defense system sale, aiming to enhance Ukrainian airspace protection.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of UAV launch corridors from Kursk, Bryansk, Oryol and Tula is essential for early warning. The effectiveness of Ukrainian air defenses against Shahed‑type drones suggests potential for further integration of Western air‑defense assets. Russian guided bomb deployments in Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia indicate a focus on disrupting Ukrainian logistics and command nodes, warranting increased reconnaissance and counter‑strike planning.
